questions.stem: This Prussian blue-stained bone marrow field (arrow) shows an erythroid precursor with a ring of iron-laden granules encircling the nucleus. Accumulation of iron in this pattern most directly results from a defect in which process?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Incorporation of iron into protoporphyrin IX to form heme

choices.label: B
choices.text: Folate-dependent nucleotide synthesis for DNA replication

explanation.summary: The image shows a ringed sideroblast. When heme synthesis is impaired (e.g., ALAS2 deficiency in X-linked sideroblastic anemia), iron cannot be incorporated into protoporphyrin IX, so unused iron accumulates in perinuclear mitochondria and stains with Prussian blue, forming a ring around the nucleus.
explanation.whyCorrect: The image shows a ringed sideroblast. When heme synthesis is impaired (e.g., ALAS2 deficiency in X-linked sideroblastic anemia), iron cannot be incorporated into protoporphyrin IX, so unused iron accumulates in perinuclear mitochondria and stains with Prussian blue, forming a ring around the nucleus.

questions.stem: This intraoperative photograph shows a strikingly black, glossy liver discovered incidentally during a cholecystectomy in a patient with mild, intermittent jaundice. This gross finding is the hallmark of which condition?

explanation.summary: Dubin-Johnson syndrome is caused by defective canalicular excretion of conjugated bilirubin (MRP2/ABCC2 mutation). Accumulation of dark pigment in hepatocytes gives the characteristic glossy black liver. Rotor syndrome is similar biochemically but does not produce a black liver.
explanation.whyCorrect: Dubin-Johnson syndrome is caused by defective canalicular excretion of conjugated bilirubin (MRP2/ABCC2 mutation). Accumulation of dark pigment in hepatocytes gives the characteristic glossy black liver. Rotor syndrome is similar biochemically but does not produce a black liver.

questions.stem: This photograph shows a neonate with jaundice receiving the depicted blue-green light treatment. By what mechanism does this therapy lower the concentration of unconjugated bilirubin?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Enhancement of bilirubin binding to serum albumin

choices.label: B
choices.text: Stimulation of heme oxygenase to accelerate heme breakdown

choices.label: C
choices.text: Photoisomerization of unconjugated bilirubin into water-soluble forms excretable without conjugation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Direct thermal degradation of bilirubin in the skin

explanation.summary: Phototherapy exposes the infant to blue-green light that isomerizes unconjugated bilirubin in skin capillaries into water-soluble photoisomers, which can be excreted in urine and bile without hepatic conjugation. It does not act by inducing UDPGT or by binding albumin.
explanation.whyCorrect: Phototherapy exposes the infant to blue-green light that isomerizes unconjugated bilirubin in skin capillaries into water-soluble photoisomers, which can be excreted in urine and bile without hepatic conjugation. It does not act by inducing UDPGT or by binding albumin.
